headline: Clear ROI Data for Corporate Sponsors
subheading: Peerceptiv turns the real work inside your executive programs into structured skill evidence, giving sponsors ROI data they demand and your program a competitive advantage.
challengeHeader: The Market Has Shifted. Differentiate your Program.
challengeIntro: UNICON's July 2025 Pulse Survey found that US executive education programs saw an 18% decrease in both custom and open enrollment demand — the steepest decline of any region globally. At the same time, more than half of exec ed leaders report increased pressure from institutional leadership on revenue and financial performance.

challengeBullets.title: Sponsors are cutting programs they cannot measure
challengeBullets.body: L&D budgets are under review everywhere. When sponsors cannot see evidence of skill change, renewal is a negotiation — not a given. Completion certificates and satisfaction scores are no longer enough to justify a six-figure investment.

challengeBullets.title: The new buying question is "what changed?"
challengeBullets.body: Corporate buyers are not asking whether participants finished. They are asking if they can apply newly learned skills. If your program cannot answer that question with data, a buyer will find one that can.

challengeBullets.title: Internal academies are closing the differentiation gap
challengeBullets.body: Corporate universities now offer many of the same topics as executive programs, but faster and cheaper. The programs that retain sponsor relationships are the ones that deliver something internal teams cannot: verified, third-party skill evidence tied to real work.

howItWorksSubheader: Peerceptiv works inside your existing courses — using the projects, presentations, and capstones you already assign — to surface real skill data you can bring to every sponsor conversation.
ctaHeading: See what Peerceptiv can do for Berkeley's Programs
ctaBody: 15 minutes. We'll show you exactly how it works for executive cohorts and answer any questions about fit.

headline: JPMorgan Develops the Best Analysts. Now You Can Prove It.
subheading: JPMorgan Chase runs some of the most competitive early career programs in financial services. Peerceptiv helps you measure whether your analysts are actually ready — not just whether they showed up.
challengeHeader: The Problem With Manager-Centric Assessment at Scale
challengeIntro: When early career cohorts span Investment Banking, Wealth Management, Commercial Banking, and Technology — each with its own managing directors and VPs reviewing project work — assessment becomes inconsistent by design. In organizations your size, a consistent pattern emerges: some analysts get rigorous, detailed feedback. Others get a checkbox. And no one can answer the question that matters most at promotion time.

challengeBullets.title: 50+ Business Lines. 50+ Different Grading Standards.
challengeBullets.body: When every managing director evaluates Summer Analysts and early career cohorts against their own internal bar, you don't have a feedback system — you have a lottery. Structured peer review enforces a consistent rubric across every division, every cohort, every geography.

challengeBullets.title: You Can See Completion. You Can't See Readiness.
challengeBullets.body: JPMorgan Chase's early career programs are built around real work — deal analysis, client case projects, analytics deliverables. That's exactly the kind of high-stakes output that deserves rigorous evaluation. But 'completed the project' and 'has the judgment to advise clients' are two very different data points. Right now, you're measuring one of them.

challengeBullets.title: Your Managing Directors Are Doing Two Jobs.
challengeBullets.body: Reviewing 30+ project submissions per cohort cycle is not a high-value use of a VP or MD's time. At firms like Accenture — running 1,500+ licenses across structured cohorts — Peerceptiv reduced grading burden by 85%. Peer review handles the volume. Your managers focus on development conversations that actually build the leadership pipeline.

howItWorksSubheader: Structured peer review that works inside JPMorgan Chase's competency framework — not instead of it.
ctaHeading: See What Peerceptiv Can Do for JPMorgan Chase's Programs
ctaBody: Peerceptiv doesn't replace your competency model or your managers. It gives you the measurement infrastructure to prove — with data — that your early career talent is ready for the next level. If you're evaluating how to scale consistent, rigorous assessment across your analyst and associate cohorts, let's talk. Accenture saw 35% better learning outcomes and 85% less grading time. Here's what that looks like in your context.
